title: Metagonia myrtifolia
taxonomy: Metagonia myrtifolia is a species of spider in the family Pholcidae. This classification places it within the arachnid order, specifically within the infraorder Araneomorphae. The family Pholcidae, also known as cellar spiders or daddy long legs, is a group of arachnids characterized by their long, thin legs and irregular web-building habits. Metagonia myrtifolia is one of the many species within this family, with its specific classification providing insight into its evolutionary relationships and behavioral traits. By understanding its classification, researchers can better comprehend the spider's ecological role and its place within the broader arachnid community.
properties: Metagonia myrtifolia is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, however, limited information is available regarding its therapeutic properties. parts: Metagonia myrtifolia, a plant species native to Central and South America, has various medicinal parts used in traditional medicine.
